claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by accessing the eligibility assessment system.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

**First, let me ask about your child:**

**How old is your child (or children)?** 

Please tell me their age(s), as eligibility depends on whether they are:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old
- 20 or over

If you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, please let me know each of their ages and I'll assess them individually.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, yes I'd like to check about Child Benefit. I've got two kids - Alex is 12 and Blake is 8.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. You have two children:
- **Alex** (age 12)
- **Blake** (age 8)

Both children are under 16, which is great as this is the first step towards eligibility. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now, the next question:

**Are Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since they're both young (12 and 8), I expect they're not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this for the assessment. This rule typically applies to older children, but I need to verify for completeness.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not working at all - they're just kids in school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's perfect! Both children are in school and not working, which meets this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving any benefits in their own right** (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

Again, since they're young children in school, this typically wouldn't apply, but I need to confirm they're not receiving benefits like ESA or Universal Credit in their own names.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't get any benefits themselves - they're just regular kids.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That's another requirement met. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake in any apprenticeships?**

Since they're 12 and 8 and in regular school, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm they're not in any type of apprenticeship program. This would include things like:
- Apprenticeships in England
- Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland
- Apprenticeships in Wales (except Foundation level, which is approved)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're way too young for apprenticeships - they're just in primary and secondary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! They're just regular school kids. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (most common situation)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above National Insurance threshold for 3 months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do any of these apply to you?** Most people simply normally live in the UK - is that your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! You meet the residency requirement by normally living in the UK (in Manchester). Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to ask about your responsibility for Alex and Blake:

**Are you responsible for Alex and Blake?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following applies:

1. **You live with the children** (most common)
2. You contribute to their upkeep by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

**Do Alex and Blake live with you?** That would be the typical situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! You live with both Alex and Blake, so you meet the responsibility requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time. If someone else is claiming but you both agree you should be the one to claim instead, that's okay - they would just need to stop their claim first.

**Is anyone else claiming for either of your children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! No one else is claiming for them. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check if there are any special circumstances regarding where the children are living:

**Are Alex and Blake:**
- Currently in hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or are they living normally at home with you?

Since you mentioned they live at home with you, I'm expecting they're in the "normal living" situation, but I need to confirm there are no special circumstances like extended hospital stays or care arrangements.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're just living normally at home with me - nothing special like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex and Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are you paying for their care yourself (rather than the council paying for it)? Since they're living at home with you normally, I expect you're supporting them yourself, but I need to confirm the council isn't contributing to their costs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - I'm paying for everything myself. The council isn't paying anything towards them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Excellent News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

### Summary for Your Children:

✅ **Alex (age 12)** - ELIGIBLE
- Under 16 years old
- Not working full-time
- Not receiving benefits
- Not in an apprenticeship
- Lives with you at home

✅ **Blake (age 8)** - ELIGIBLE
- Under 16 years old
- Not working full-time
- Not receiving benefits
- Not in an apprenticeship
- Lives with you at home

### Why You're Eligible:
You meet all the requirements as a claimant:
- ✅ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ You're responsible for both children (they live with you)
- ✅ No one else is claiming for them
- ✅ They live normally at home with you
- ✅ The council isn't paying for their care

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates if applicable) for Alex and Blake

### Important Things to Know:

💰 **Payment rates:**
- You'll receive Child Benefit for both children
- Payments are typically made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge:**
- If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through a tax charge
- You can still claim and choose not to receive payments if this applies

**Ready to apply?** Visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Do you have any questions about the next steps or anything else?

reasonings: Alex (age 12) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) Claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them; (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) Claimant lives with Blake and is responsible for them; (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Blake lives normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children (Alex, age 12, and Blake, age 8). Both children meet all the qualifying child criteria as they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. The claimant meets all the claimant criteria: normally resident in the UK (Manchester), responsible for both children by living with them, no other person claiming, children living normally at home, and the council is not paying towards their care.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 12) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) Claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them; (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) Claimant lives with Blake and is responsible for them; (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Blake lives normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children (Alex, age 12, and Blake, age 8). Both children meet all the qualifying child criteria as they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. The claimant meets all the claimant criteria: normally resident in the UK (Manchester), responsible for both children by living with them, no other person claiming, children living normally at home, and the council is not paying towards their care.
